product_overview: SIGERD enables instructors to report incidents with photographic evidence, administrators to manage users and convert incidents into assignable tasks, and workers to execute and close tasks with role-based dashboards and secure authentication.
core_goals: Provide secure authentication and role-based access (Admin, Instructor, Worker).
Allow Instructors to report incidents with validated photographic evidence and track their status.
Enable Admins to manage users and convert incidents into tasks, assign workers, review progress and export reports.
Allow Workers to receive, update and complete assigned tasks with clear status transitions.
Enforce file, authorization and input validation rules and surface clear validation/errors to users.
Support notifications, session management and performance for large datasets (pagination/lazy loading).

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Secure login, session handling and basic profile access. Entry point for all users.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill email and password -> Click login -> Redirect to /dashboard -> See role-appropriate dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Fill email and password (invalid) -> Click login -> See credential error message and remain on /login
Navigate to /login -> Fill email and password -> Wait for CSRF token expiry -> Click login -> See 419 Page Expired or validation error

features.name: Role-Based Dashboards
features.description: Dashboard landing that redirects users to role-specific dashboards and shows overview metrics.
user_flows: Login as Admin -> Redirect to /dashboard -> System redirects to /admin/dashboard -> View overview metrics
Login as Instructor -> Redirect to /dashboard -> System redirects to /instructor/dashboard -> View overview metrics
Login as Worker -> Redirect to /dashboard -> System redirects to /worker/dashboard -> View overview metrics
Login as Instructor -> Attempt to navigate to /admin/dashboard -> See 403 Forbidden or redirect to /dashboard

features.name: User Management (Admin)
features.description: Admins can create, read, update and delete system users via the admin users UI.
user_flows: Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/users -> Click Create New User -> Fill user details -> Click Save -> See new user in the users list
Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/users -> Click Edit on a user -> Modify fields -> Click Save -> See updated details
Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/users -> Click Delete on a user -> Confirm deletion -> See user removed from list (soft delete)
Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /admin/users -> See 403 Forbidden or access denied message
Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/users -> Click Create New User -> Leave required fields blank -> Click Save -> See validation errors on required fields

features.name: Incident Management (Instructor & Admin)
features.description: Instructors report incidents with evidence; they view and follow status updates. Admins review incidents and can convert them to tasks.
user_flows: Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /instructor/incidents -> Click 'Report Incident' -> Fill title -> Fill description -> Fill location -> Attach 1 valid image under 2MB -> Click Submit -> See confirmation 'pending review' and incident listed

Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /instructor/incidents -> Click 'Report Incident' -> Fill title and location -> Do not attach any image -> Click Submit -> See validation error 'Must provide at least one image' and incident not created
Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /instructor/incidents -> Click 'Report Incident' -> Attach image larger than 2MB -> Click Submit -> See file size validation error and incident not created
Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /instructor/incidents -> Click 'Report Incident' -> Attach non-image file (.php or .exe) -> Click Submit -> See MIME/type validation error and rejection
Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /instructor/incidents -> See list of incidents -> Confirm list shows only incidents owned by logged-in instructor

Login as Instructor -> Navigate to /instructor/incidents -> Manually change incident id in URL to another user's id -> Attempt to load edit -> See 403 Forbidden or 404 blocked by authorization policies

Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/tasks -> Open an incident entry -> Click 'Convert to Task' -> Assign to a Worker -> Click Save -> See task created and incident status updated to 'Assigned' and notification queued to the Instructor

features.name: Task Management (Admin & Worker)
features.description: Admins create/assign tasks from incidents and review completed work; Workers view and update their assigned tasks.
user_flows: Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/tasks -> Click Create/Convert Task -> Select incident -> Assign to Worker -> Click Save -> See new task in admin task list with status 'Assigned'
Login as Admin -> Navigate to /admin/tasks -> Filter completed tasks -> Click Export to PDF -> Receive downloadable PDF of selected tasks
Login as Worker -> Navigate to /worker/tasks -> See tasks assigned to authenticated worker -> Click task to open detail -> Click 'Mark In-Progress' -> See task status update to 'In-Progress'
Login as Worker -> Navigate to /worker/tasks -> Open an in-progress task -> Click 'Mark Completed' -> See task status update to 'Completed' and notification sent to Admin and reporting Instructor
Login as Worker -> Navigate to /worker/tasks -> Attempt to update a task not assigned to self -> See 403 Forbidden or validation error preventing update

features.name: Profile & Settings
features.description: Users can update profile information, change password, and toggle appearance preferences (light/dark).
user_flows: Login -> Redirect to /dashboard -> Click Profile link -> Update personal fields and upload avatar -> Click Save -> See confirmation and updated profile rendered
Login -> Redirect to /dashboard -> Click Profile link -> Enter current password -> Enter new password -> Click Save -> See success confirmation indicating password changed
Login -> Redirect to /dashboard -> Click Profile link -> Modify hidden role input in DOM -> Click Save -> Backend ignores role field and role remains unchanged

features.name: Notifications
features.description: System notifies users about incident assignment and resolution; notifications mark as read when opened.
user_flows: Login as Admin -> Convert incident to Task and assign Worker -> System enqueues notification -> Instructor receives notification count increment
Instructor -> Click received notification -> System redirects to the incident detail view -> Notification is marked as read and badge count decrements
Worker -> Mark task as Completed -> System sends notification to Admin and Instructor -> Recipients see incoming notification and can click to view related task or incident
Keep notification stale (do not click) until session expires -> After session expiration attempt to open notification -> System redirects to /login with 'Session expired' message

features.name: Role-Based Dashboards
features.description: Different dashboards based on user role (Admin, Instructor, Worker)

features.name: User Management
features.description: CRUD for system users by Administrator

features.name: Incident Management
features.description: Instructors report incidents, Admins review them and can convert to Tasks

features.name: Task Management
features.description: Admins assign tasks to Workers, Workers update the status of tasks
user_interactions: Update task status to in-progress or completed
